incorrects.description: Student selected 2 groups of 2: miscounted both groups and items

params.description: All circles briefly highlight to draw attention to the full set.

steps.text: Count all the circles. Then look inside one circle and count the dots.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 groups of 2: counted groups correctly but miscounted items per group

params.description: One circle pulses to draw focus.

steps.text: You counted the circles. Now look inside one circle. Count the dots carefully.

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 groups of 3: miscounted groups but counted items correctly

steps.text: Count the circles one at a time. How many circles are there?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

steps.text: Let me show you. First, I count the circles: 1, 2, 3. Three circles. Now I look inside one circle and count the dots: 1, 2, 3. Three dots in each circle. So we have 3 groups of 3. That's what equal groups are: the same number in each group.
